_comment: Per-type backward-walk speed demo (#461): a Spearmen regiment and an Archers regiment are staged side by side (drill mode, no opponent), both facing SOUTH, both forced to Normal formation (so both share the same grid spacing -- otherwise Spearmen's own TIGHT default and Archers' own LOOSE default would confound the comparison with a formation-density difference on top of the back-speed one). Both are given a drilled rear-move order at the same tick, so each executes an identical-shape about-face and rear-rank countermarch reform (see 'Backing up costs time' in website/tactics.qmd) over the same crossing distance. The two types differ only in back_speed_fraction (Spearmen 0.35 vs Archers 0.55, from Battle._default_loadout's back_fraction) and jog_mps, so the settling-time gap on screen (and in the state dump's per-unit order_phase/current_speed) is attributable to the per-type backward pace, not to a formation-density difference. The lighter-kitted, faster-backing Archers reform and step off first; the heavier-kitted, slower-backing Spearmen still hold position later in the clip. Driven live through the real SelectionManager by tools/demo/DemoInputRecorder.gd; world coordinates; physics ticks (60/s).
